article_items.title: Nvidia Signals $150B Spend in Taiwan
article_items.summary: Nvidia Signals $150B Spend in Taiwan Speaking at a launch event for Nvidia’s upcoming Taiwan headquarters, CEO Jensen Huang deemed the country the “epicenter” of the AI revolution Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said the AI chipmaker will spend up to $150 billion a year in Taiwan going forward. The company previously revealed plans for a $50 billion investment in AI in the U.S. The company previously revealed plans for a $50 billion investment in AI in the U.S. The AI Summit London is the flagship AI event of London Tech Week, bringing together enterprise leaders, technical buyers and innovators to turn AI breakthroughs into real business impact. Now, we're going to spend $100 billion, $150 billion in Taiwan each year.” “Taiwan is the epicenter of the AI revolution, this is where the chips come, packaging comes.

key_points: Nvidia Signals $150B Spend in Taiwan Speaking at a launch event for Nvidia’s upcoming Taiwan headquarters, CEO Jensen Huang deemed the country the “epicenter” of the AI revolution Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said the AI.
The company previously revealed plans for a $50 billion investment in AI in the U.S.
Now, we're going to spend $100 billion, $150 billion in Taiwan each year.” “Taiwan is the epicenter of the AI revolution, this is where the chips come, packaging comes.
The investment plans come as Taiwan takes an increased share of Nvidia’s revenue, rising more than 50% year-over-year in the latest quarter.
The annual $150 billion spend would be one of Nvidia’s largest financial commitments to date.
Dubbed Constellation, the site is anticipated to be open by 2030 and accommodate up to 4,000 employees.
Last week, AMD revealed a $10 billion investment in Taiwan’s AI sector to support the development of advanced AI chips.
“Four years, five years ago, Nvidia was spending about $105 billion a year in Taiwan.
Huang made his comments in Taipei at an event on May 26 to preview Nvidia’s new Taiwan headquarters.
“ Taiwan is booming ,” Huang said.
over a four-year period, $125 billion per year.
“This is where AI supercomputers were created.” The vendor did not disclose details on the length of time Nvidia will invest in Taiwan.
